div.style: display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); line-height: 1.2; vertical-align: top; width: 604px; white-space: normal; text-align: center;

div.div: (if new): description, venue, is public, start, end, URLs, course areas

div.span: (if new): description, display name, reverse order, overall leaderboard

div.div: leaderboard/regatta: type (regatta/flexible), name, display name, discarding rules, scoring system, course area
(if regatta): boat class, start, end, start time inference, ranking
series: name, options, fleets (name, color, rank), races (name)

div.style: display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); line-height: 1.2; vertical-align: top; white-space: nowrap; text-align: center;

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); line-height: 1.2; vertical-align: top; overflow: hidden; max-height: 355px; max-width: 721px; width: 721px; white-space: normal; text-align: center;

div.span: To keep things tidy, not all navigational edges are shown.

div.div: Once the user may navigate freely between all screens at all times (navigation at the top: Step1>Step2>...>Review). This is possible because we can provide sensible defaults everywhere, once name, boat class and number of races have been in the first screen.
The following usage scenarios should be covered (have a mental run-through, what is missing?):
3. change scoring system and have split fleets
4. add act to event series (new event and leaderboard, pre-populated with data from existing ones in that leaderboard group)

div.span: - handle (re-use/create) multiple leaderboards/regattas in one go: maybe it's OK to open the wizard multiple times?

div.div: - handle multiple leaderboard groups or events -> again, we could let the user create a whole hierarchy (several events, with severeal leaderboard groups each, with several regattas each), but that might defeat the purpose of the wizard
- smartphone-tracking related scenarios: register competitors, divide competitor pool into split fleets within series

The idea of the wizard is to allow the user to finish at any time, if he is satisfied with the level of detail. Sensible defaults should be used for all the following steps that are skipped. Steps should become increasingly irrelevant (exception: the last review step).
I would have liked to have the leaderboard/regatta step as second, before dabbling with leaderboard groups and events. However
a) to support the scenario "new act in event series" the user must first choose what leaderboard to copy
b) the course area set in the regatta/leaderboard depends on the event (we could work with a default, and the user could jump back later on if he e.g. wants to create a new course area and use that).

is it worth connecting this part?
will somebody use the wizard to add races to an existing regatta (or flexible leaderboard)?
